# Lease-service: maxActiveHoldMs fairness bound + wire shouldYieldHeavyMaintenanceLease for holders

Sub of #14144 (heavy-maintenance lease fairness). The **lease-service side** of the time-driven cooperative yield.

## Context

The yield primitive already exists but is unconsumed: `shouldYieldHeavyMaintenanceLease(lease, {maxActiveHoldMs})` (`HeavyMaintenanceLeaseService.mjs:121`, pure/read-only, `held > maxActiveHoldMs`) + `acquireHeavyMaintenanceLeaseSync` / `releaseHeavyMaintenanceLeaseSync`. What's missing: the **policy value** (`maxActiveHoldMs` has no config leaf) and a **holder-facing wrapper** so the kbSync yield-point (sub #B, @neo-opus-grace) consults it without threading config at the call-site.

## Scope

1. Add `orchestrator.heavyMaintenance.maxActiveHoldMs` reactive config leaf (`config.template.mjs`, read at the use-site per ADR-0019) ŌĆö the fairness bound. Sized to interleave a multi-minute `githubWorkflowSync` without thrashing the heavy mutex (e.g. ~30ŌĆō60 min default; falsy ŌćÆ never-yields back-compat, which `shouldYieldHeavyMaintenanceLease` already honors).
2. A thin service method (e.g. `Orchestrator#shouldYieldHeavyLease(lease)` or a lease-service instance method) that reads the leaf + delegates to the pure `shouldYieldHeavyMaintenanceLease`.
3. Unit test (pure: lease + bound ŌåÆ boolean; the primitive's degrade-on-falsy path).

## Contract Ledger

| Surface | Signature | Consumer | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| service yield-check | `shouldYieldHeavyLease(lease) ŌåÆ Boolean` | sub #B kbSync yield-point | reads `maxActiveHoldMs` leaf; delegates to pure `shouldYieldHeavyMaintenanceLease(lease,{maxActiveHoldMs})` |
| config leaf | `orchestrator.heavyMaintenance.maxActiveHoldMs` (number, env-overridable, falsyŌćÆnever-yield) | the service method | the fairness policy value |

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `maxActiveHoldMs` reactive config leaf exists + read at the use-site (not pass-through).
- [ ] A holder-facing method delegates to the existing pure primitive; no behavior change until a holder consumes it.
- [ ] Unit test covers yield-true (held > bound), yield-false (within bound), and falsy-bound never-yield.
- [ ] Does NOT auto-close the epic ŌĆö sub #B (kbSync yield-point) makes it behavioral (the #14175 surface-vs-consume lesson).

Refs #14144 (epic), #14146 (resumable kbSync that makes the yield safe), #14161.

### @neo-opus-ada - 2026-06-27T04:01:36Z

## Follow-up surfaced by @neo-opus-grace's #14186 V-B-A: extract the pure lease primitives

kbSync's `VectorService` runs as a SUBPROCESS; importing `HeavyMaintenanceLeaseService.mjs` to reach `shouldYieldHeavyMaintenanceLease` would pull in `Neo` + `Base` (the orchestrator stack) ŌĆö a layering violation. So #14186's consumer can't import the service directly.

**Structural-pre-flight (done):**
- Neo/Base in `HeavyMaintenanceLeaseService.mjs` is used ONLY by the thin class wrapper (`class ŌĆ” extends Base` + `Neo.setupClass`). The ~13 exported functions (`toTimestamp`, `isPidAlive`, `isLeaseStale`, `shouldYieldHeavyMaintenanceLease`, `buildLeasePayload`, `acquire`/`releaseHeavyMaintenanceLeaseSync`, ŌĆ”) are PURE (crypto/fs/path) ŌĆö **Neo/Base-free**, so extractable.
- Placement: `ai/services/shared/lease/heavyMaintenanceLeasePrimitives.mjs` ŌĆö matches the existing `ai/services/shared/vector/chromaClientPrimitives.mjs` convention; VectorService already imports from `shared/` (e.g. `DestructiveOperationGuard`).
- Backward-compat: `HeavyMaintenanceLeaseService.mjs` re-exports the primitives (`export * from ŌĆ”`), so existing orchestrator importers are unaffected; #14186's VectorService imports the pure module directly (no Neo/Base pull-in).

**Plan:** move the pure functions ŌåÆ the shared primitives module; service re-exports; HeavyMaintenanceLeaseService specs stay green (they exercise the same functions). A ~580-line structural move ŌĆö driving it on a fresh cycle (careful refactor, not a rush), coordinating the final shape with @neo-opus-grace (#14186 consumer). Teed up here. ŌĆö Ada

- 2026-06-27T06:52:49Z @tobiu referenced in commit `fc3532d` - "feat(ai): heavyMaintenance.maxActiveHoldMs lease-fairness leaf (#14185) (#14187)

The bound on continuous heavy-maintenance-lease hold: a long-running heavy task yields after maxActiveHoldMs so a starved heavy peer (githubWorkflowSync, which else stales the sandman handoff ~20h behind a full re-embed) interleaves; the next sweep re-acquires. Supplies the missing policy value for the already-built, currently-unconsumed shouldYieldHeavyMaintenanceLease primitive. Substrate-first for #14186 (the kbSync yield-point consumer) ŌĆö inert until consumed. Default HOUR_MS bounds the ~20h starvation to <=1h with minimal yield-churn; env-overridable. Mechanism is pre-tested (HeavyMaintenanceLeaseService.spec); SSOT lint covers the leaf.
